posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
Predictions: 0 of 11 Winners, 0 Perfect, 0 Points
|
Tied for 51st

Galanti vs. Clayton
Austin Clayton, TKO, R3
This is a banger, both solid prospects and both grapplers. Galanti put up a good fight against Holmes but the fight was weird. He made it very competitive with his takedowns but he fell behind on the feet and looked very uncomfortable at times. He’s dominated since then but has also fought mostly guys who can’t grapple wel or are strikers. Clayton has similar flaws though, his fight against Huckeby he was getting touched up a ton before he broke the guy down. He seems to take punches better than galanti and has more confidence than him, which could go a long way since Clayton may be able to counter the wrestling. Winner should go to dwcs

Metzgar vs. Romero
Max Metzgar, SUB, R3
I remember Metzgar getting hype even as an amateur back when he signed with Bellator. He hasn’t fought the best comp but has continued to win since his pro debut. This seems like another favorable fight on paper but I expect him to show out

Akhmadov vs. Lleshi
Xhelal Lleshi, TKO, R3
I wish they gave lleshi a tougher fight but I imagine not many want to fight him. I’m very impressed with him, he gave Hasan the toughest fight of his career (shouldn’t have been a split tho, classic damage vs control fight). Since then he has dominated everyone he’s fought, showing a well rounded game. I think he has a bright future, him and Hasan are two of the best guys in Cffc

Ikei vs. Wilcox
Rosalani Ikei, SUB, R2
Not the best time for ikei to debut given her brother recently lost but im interested to see how she looks. I assume this is a layup for her

Morales vs. Ramsay
Manny Morales, DEC
Ramsay is a bum. He’s like mid 30s too so his ufc window is gone; he did get screwed out of a tuf shot tho which is unfortunate.

Fiore vs. Lewis
Richie Lewis, DEC
Really cool fight, Lewis getting the ex ufc test likely means a big opportunity is next. I’ve always been impressed with him but the last few years haven’t been the best. He’s on the older side now and the Miranda fight was a big setback. He looked really good against orozco but it was another wrestling heavy performance. His wrestling is very high level but it’s all he has. Fiore had a terrible ufc run but he also got match made horribly and never should’ve been there to begin with. He hasn’t fought anyone great but has been finishing everyone. He’s always been a good grappler so if he is improving his striking/wrestling maybe he could win. I think it’s gong to be very difficult to stop the wrestling onslaught but I’m rooting for fiore; would be cool if he gets an upset and gets another ufc shot, he’s one of the few NE fighters who isn’t a complete can

Aliev vs. Palmer
Adam Aliev, TKO, R2
Good fight. Taking a gamble with Aliev. He looked good in ACA fights but fought nodobies. He hasn’t fought in a while but is still super young and training in the U.S. now. Palmer is a huge step up in comp but he too is a grappler and I haven’t been that impressed with him thus far

posts.author: flavorpacket
posts.content:
galanti vs. clayton
galanti, dec

interesting fight as both are good prospects. galanti reminds me of ramiz brahimaj for whatever reason, they look alike and kind of fight the same way. clayton looks promising but untested, he's smashing guys who aren't on galanti's level. this fight should look like two guys with very similar styles butting heads for 5 rounds. could see this being very competitive and i have to lean galanti considering he's more proven at this level. wouldn't be surprised if clayton got the upset but i feel like this should be a greasy split decision type of fight for the local guy.

posts.author: Mazeflare
posts.content: I'm not surprised, the Richie Miranda fight really exposed Lewis is nothing but a wrestler in MMA with way too much control and afraid to take risks to find a finish and if there is one thing Fiore was good at it was the jiu jitsu
